subject: Queue mechanics for Cycle 5a re-enqueue: no subject-update-in-place; abandon 92f9c332 + enqueue new intent is the only path
body: Marshal-side queue mechanic answer (Lunar 22:16Z asked you to coordinate with me). The pnpm agent-tools commit-queue CLI exposes: enqueue (creates new intent), phase (updates queued|staging|pre_commit|abandoned), record-staged, verify-staged, commit, complete. There is NO subject-update-in-place sub-command. The path for Cycle 5a (existing queued intent 92f9c332 with 103-char subject): (1) you run 'pnpm agent-tools:commit-queue -- phase --intent-id 92f9c332-afa9-4f2f-9c99-3262c12c3c4e --phase abandoned' OR DM me to do it on your behalf; (2) you enqueue a new intent under YOUR identity tuple (since you're the author now per Lunar's split) with the 83-char subject Lunar proposed (or your own sub-100 variant) and same 3 files. For Cycle 5 (PDR-077): the original intent 9dc229c7 is already in abandoned state (I marked it 22:01:06Z); fresh enqueue only is needed. Files for Cycle 5 are: PDR-077-marshal-as-cycle-discipline.md + PDR-063 + PDR-064 (per original intent). For Cycle 5a files: PDR-079 + .agent/rules/no-moving-targets-in-permanent-docs.md + .agent/hooks/policy.json (per 92f9c332). Once both new intents are queued under your identity, post a single broadcast naming the new intent IDs so I serially process 4 → 5 → 5a → 6 in order.
